skill.name: quiz-generator
skill.description: Generate 50-question interactive quizzes with balanced answer distribution, immediate feedback, source attribution, and validation checklists for chapter assessments.
skill.summary: Creates balanced interactive chapter quizzes with feedback and quality checks.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-severity hits, but the Markdown hits are false positives caused by code fences, inline examples, and educational quiz text. The real risk is moderate: bundled Python scripts can rewrite a user-supplied quiz file, and the skill instructs agents to run them with Bash/Write access.

medium_findings.title: User-Supplied File Rewrite Script
medium_findings.description: The bundled redistribution scripts read a path from command-line input and write modified quiz content back to that same path. This is intended behavior for quiz files, but a mistaken or malicious invocation could overwrite another local Markdown-like file because the script does not restrict paths to a project quiz directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write operation is direct and uses a command-line file path. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but the absence of path constraints creates a real local file modification risk.

medium_findings.title: Bash and Write Tooling Required for Normal Use
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash and Write access and tells the agent to run the redistribution script after quiz generation. This is not malicious, but it expands the impact of bad input or an incorrect path because normal operation includes local command execution and file writes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The declared tools and run instructions are explicit in the skill file. The risk depends on runtime use, so confidence is high for capability exposure but not for malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings in Markdown files are false positives. The flagged content is fenced code, inline formatting, filenames, checklist examples, or answer-distribution examples rather than Ruby or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown fences or inline code markers. They are not executable Ruby backticks and do not create a command injection path.

low_findings.title: Security Keywords in Educational Quiz Text
low_findings.description: The SAM, weak-crypto, C2, and reconnaissance static hits are false positives from educational prose and spreadsheet formulas. No evidence found that these lines access sensitive files, implement cryptography, contact command-and-control infrastructure, or perform reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are sample quiz explanations and a spreadsheet range formula. The scanner appears to match substrings such as SAME or MIN rather than dangerous behavior.

low_findings.title: Unused Subprocess Import and Comment
low_findings.description: The v2 script imports subprocess and comments about calling Claude from the command line, but no subprocess call is present in the implementation. This should be cleaned up to reduce reviewer concern, but no command execution path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The import and comment are suspicious in isolation, but the inspected code path falls back to local string replacement and does not call subprocess.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Overwrite via Script Argument
dangerous_patterns.description: Both redistribution scripts accept a path argument and write rewritten quiz content to that path. Users should review the target path before running the script.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write calls are direct and confirmed. The behavior is expected, but it is still a dangerous pattern when combined with arbitrary path input.

content.value_statement: Long chapter quizzes are hard to keep fair, complete, and consistent. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to build interactive quiz banks with balanced answers and review checks.
actual_capabilities: Generates 50-question chapter quiz banks for an existing Quiz component format.
Prompts for conceptual questions that test application rather than recall.
Balances correct answers across option indices using documented A-H sequences.
Requires immediate feedback explanations that address correct and incorrect options.
Adds source attribution for each question to connect answers back to lessons.
Provides checklists for option length, naming, formatting, and distribution review.
limitations: Assumes the project already has a compatible Quiz component.
The redistribution scripts are designed for the documented quiz structure only.
Option length validation still requires manual counting and review.
Generated questions need subject-matter review before student use.

use_cases.title: Build a Course Chapter Quiz
use_cases.description: Create a 50-question quiz from lesson material with conceptual questions, feedback, and lesson source references.

use_cases.title: Normalize Answer Distribution
use_cases.description: Reorder correct answers so students cannot infer answers from repeated positions or simple patterns.

use_cases.title: Review Assessment Quality
use_cases.description: Check option length parity, explanation quality, component formatting, and file naming before publishing.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Basic Quiz
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a single chapter
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 50-question interactive quiz for Chapter [number]: [topic]. Use the Quiz component format, four options per question, immediate feedback, and lesson source labels.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Conceptual Depth
prompt_templates.scenario: Refine a draft that has too many recall questions
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this draft quiz and rewrite recall questions into application-based scenarios. Keep the same chapter scope and maintain four balanced options per question.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Distribution and Length
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this quiz for answer distribution, option length spread, repeated correct-answer positions, source fields, and explanation completeness. List only required fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Finalize a Production Quiz
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for a complete publish-ready pass
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a final chapter quiz package from these lessons. Generate 50 questions, apply an A-H distribution sequence, verify option parity, and provide a concise handoff checklist.

output_examples.input: Chapter on Python variables and data types
output_examples.output: A chapter assessment with scenario-based questions, four plausible options, balanced correct answers, and feedback that explains the concept behind each choice.

output_examples.input: Draft quiz with too many correct answers in option C
output_examples.output: A revised quiz plan that redistributes correct answers, identifies explanations needing updates, and flags any repeated answer-position patterns.
A checklist report covering question count, source labels, option length parity, answer distribution, component format, and filename convention.

best_practices: Provide the full chapter lessons so questions cover every major concept.
Run a manual review after redistribution to confirm explanations still match answers.
Keep each option similar in length so students cannot guess from wording patterns.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill for static Markdown quizzes without the expected Quiz component.
Do not publish generated questions without expert review for accuracy and fairness.
Do not run redistribution scripts on files outside the intended quiz path.

faq.question: Does this skill create static quizzes?
faq.answer: No. It targets interactive quizzes that use the documented Quiz component.

faq.question: How many questions does it expect?
faq.answer: It is designed for 50-question banks, with 15 to 20 questions shown per session.

faq.question: Can it balance answer choices?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes answer distribution guidance and scripts for A-H sequence balancing.

faq.question: Does it verify subject accuracy?
faq.answer: No. It structures and reviews quizzes, but subject experts must verify correctness.

faq.question: Why are option lengths checked?
faq.answer: Similar option lengths reduce guessing based on unusually long or short answers.

faq.question: Is it safe to run the included scripts?
faq.answer: Use care. The scripts rewrite the file path you provide, so confirm the target file first.
